prompt: Either P is necessary or C is necessary
hint: Think about what world(s) this statement is claiming something about. Is it all worlds, some worlds, or the actual world?
explanation: Two separate necessity claims joined by 'or': (□P ∨ □C). This is much stronger than □(P ∨ C) — 'either P is necessary or C is necessary' says one of them holds in EVERY world, not merely that their disjunction does.
